title: Device V2 real collection acceptance fine-grained store phases
releaseGate: Publish only after Batch 002 has produced a confirmed DB-derived imported sample set and the user approves controller-detect based real collection.
granularityRule: Single-device store is proven first with one device and one phase per story. After that, dedicated parallel stories may process all 5 confirmed cloned devices, still split into start, polling, and classification phases. Collection protocol is selected by controller detect; stories must not force SNMP/SSH/WinRM/DC2.

stories.title: Store readiness gate for one DB-derived imported device
stories.scope: For one imported D2LA device code, open the management UI or call the existing readiness endpoint, verify whether store/start is allowed, confirm credential refs and access_points are ready for controller detect, and write a compact readiness artifact for the next story.
stories.nonGoals: Do not start store, do not retry, do not analyze observations, and do not process multiple unrelated devices.
acceptance: Evidence includes the selected device code, original code mapping, endpoint/access_points/credential-ref presence, detect readiness, and readiness response.
Writes `readiness.json` with pass/block status and the exact next device code.
If blocked, classifies missing credential ref, missing endpoint, UI/API error, permission issue, or unknown blocker.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Store start for one readiness-passed device
stories.scope: Consume `readiness.json`, trigger store/start for exactly one readiness-passed D2LA device from the management UI when possible, and persist the returned task_id for follow-up stories. Collection protocol is selected by controller detect.
stories.nonGoals: Do not poll observations deeply, do not retry, and do not run cleanup.
acceptance: Store/start request and response are captured with secrets redacted, and the evidence notes whether UI or same-session API was used.
Writes `store-task-id.txt` and `store-start.json`.
If no task_id is returned, records the exact API/UI blocker and stops.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Store task summary and runs polling for one task
stories.scope: Consume `store-task-id.txt`, load task summary and runs only, and record whether the task is completed, pending, failed, or too long-running for this turn.
stories.nonGoals: Do not trigger retry, do not inspect observations/DC2, and do not restart collection.
acceptance: Writes `store-summary.json` and `store-runs.json`.
If the task is still pending near the time budget, writes current status and exits with a clear next polling step.
If failed, records failure stage/message without broad debugging.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Store observations and latest DC2 evidence classification
stories.scope: Consume task summary/runs artifacts, load observations and latest DC2 for the same task/device, and classify whether real collection evidence is present.
stories.nonGoals: Do not start or retry store, do not edit devices, and do not do broad backend repairs.
acceptance: Writes `store-observations.json`, `latest-dc2.json`, and `collection-classification.md`.
Classification is one of: real-observations-present, dc2-present, business-empty, credential-blocked, network-blocked, backend-contract-blocked, environment-blocked, unknown.
If evidence is empty, explains the next smallest follow-up story instead of expanding scope.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Collection plans and retry decision for one store task
stories.scope: Consume prior store artifacts, load collection plans, and decide whether retry is warranted. Only execute retry if the prior evidence clearly supports it and approval boundaries allow it.
stories.nonGoals: Do not perform final launch reporting, do not delete devices, and do not retry multiple tasks.
acceptance: Writes `collection-plans.json` and `retry-decision.md`.
If retry is executed, writes `retry-task-id.txt` and the next story must poll it separately.
If retry is not executed, records why retry is unnecessary, unsafe, or blocked.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

outputs: retry-task-id.txt only if retry is executed

stories.title: Parallel store start for five DB-derived imported devices
stories.scope: After the single-device store path is proven, consume the confirmed sample set and start store collection for all 5 cloned devices. Use controller detect; do not force protocols.
stories.nonGoals: Do not poll deeply, do not classify observations, and do not retry in this story.
acceptance: Writes `parallel-store-start.json` with per-device start result and task_id when available.
Records any per-device blocker without stopping unrelated device starts unless the environment is globally blocked.
All evidence redacts secrets and only records credential refs.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Parallel store task polling for five devices
stories.scope: Consume `parallel-store-start.json`, poll task summary/runs for the 5 started tasks within the time budget, and persist compact per-task status.
stories.nonGoals: Do not retry, do not do broad debugging, and do not perform final launch reporting.
acceptance: Writes `parallel-store-status.json` with task status and run summary for each device.
If tasks remain pending, writes next polling step instead of expanding scope.
Classifies global environment blockage separately from per-device blockage.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

stories.title: Parallel collection evidence classification for five devices
stories.scope: Consume `parallel-store-status.json`, load observations/latest DC2 where available, and classify collection evidence for all 5 cloned devices.
stories.nonGoals: Do not start new store tasks, do not retry, and do not edit or delete devices.
acceptance: Writes `parallel-collection-classification.md` and compact JSON evidence for observations/DC2.
Each device is classified as real-observations-present, dc2-present, business-empty, credential-blocked, network-blocked, backend-contract-blocked, environment-blocked, or unknown.
Provides the smallest next follow-up story for any unknown or blocked result.
validation: No code validation required unless files are changed.

handoffPolicy.rule: Each story consumes only the previous compact artifacts and writes the next compact handoff. Do not combine readiness, start, polling, observation/DC2 classification, plans, retry decision, and retry execution in one worker turn.
handoffPolicy.fallbackHealthGate: npm run smoke:d2-real-ops or D2P2 local fixture smoke may be run only to prove local harness health; it is not real-device proof.

stopConditions: missing credential refs or access_points needed by controller detect
selected cloned device endpoint is unreachable or readiness blocks collection
store/start does not return a task_id
task remains pending/long-running near worker budget; persist status and stop
observations/latest DC2 are empty without a business-empty classification
retry would hide credential/network/backend blockers or requires human approval
any action would touch an original non-D2LA device or perform destructive cleanup
